data.content:
### Build Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.0.1/template/page.mdx

Builds a sandbox template from a Dockerfile. It supports specifying the Dockerfile path, template name, start command, team ID, and build arguments. It also allows configuration of CPU and memory for the sandbox.

```bash
e2b template build [options] [template]

Options:
  -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
  -d, --dockerfile <file>: specify path to Dockerfile. By default E2B tries to find e2b.Dockerfile or Dockerfile in root directory.
  -n, --name <template-name>: specify sandbox template name. You can use the template name to start the sandbox with SDK. The template name must be lowercase and contain only letters, numbers, dashes and underscores.
  -c, --cmd <start-command>: specify command that will be executed when the sandbox is started.
  -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
  --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory.
  --cpu-count <cpu-count>: specify the number of CPUs that will be used to run the sandbox. The default value is 2.
  --memory-mb <memory-mb>: specify the amount of memory in megabytes that will be used to run the sandbox. Must be an even number. The default value is 512.
  --build-arg <args...>: specify additional build arguments for the build command. The format should be <varname>=<value>.
```

--------------------------------

### Create a New Sandbox (Default Template)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.8.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance using the default 'base' sandbox template. This is the simplest way to get a new sandbox environment up and running. It returns a Promise that resolves to the newly created sandbox instance.

```typescript
static create<S>(this: S, opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>

// Example usage:
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.11.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance. You can specify a template, timeout, environment variables, and other configurations for the sandbox.

```APIDOC
## POST /sandboxes

### Description
Creates a new sandbox. By default, the sandbox is created from the default `base` sandbox template.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/sandboxes

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **template** (string) - Optional - Sandbox template name or ID
- **timeout** (integer) - Optional - Timeout for the sandbox in seconds, default to 300 seconds. Maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86,400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3,600 seconds) for Hobby users.
- **metadata** (object) - Optional - Custom metadata for the sandbox
- **envs** (object) - Optional - Custom environment variables for the sandbox
- **api_key** (string) - Optional - E2B API Key to use for authentication, defaults to `E2B_API_KEY` environment variable
- **domain** (string) - Optional - The domain to use for the sandbox
- **debug** (boolean) - Optional - Enable debug mode for the sandbox
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds
- **proxy** (object) - Optional - Proxy to use for the request and for the requests made to the returned sandbox
- **secure** (boolean) - Optional - Secure the sandbox with an access token
- **allow_internet_access** (boolean) - Optional - Allow sandbox to access the internet, defaults to `True`

### Request Example
```json
{
  "template": "node",
  "timeout": 600,
  "envs": {
    "MY_VAR": "my_value"
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (201)
- **sandbox_id** (string) - The ID of the newly created sandbox
- **template** (string) - The template used for the sandbox
- **status** (string) - The status of the sandbox

### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandbox_id": "sbx_abcdef123456",
  "template": "node",
  "status": "running"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### create

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.1/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance.

```APIDOC
## POST /sandbox/create

### Description
Creates a new sandbox instance. By default, the sandbox is created from the default `base` sandbox template.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/sandbox/create

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **template** (string) - Optional - Sandbox template name or ID.
- **timeout** (integer) - Optional - Timeout for the sandbox in seconds, default to 300 seconds. Maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86_400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3_600 seconds) for Hobby users.
- **metadata** (object) - Optional - Custom metadata for the sandbox. Keys and values must be strings.
- **envs** (object) - Optional - Custom environment variables for the sandbox. Keys and values must be strings.
- **api_key** (string) - Optional - E2B API Key to use for authentication, defaults to `E2B_API_KEY` environment variable.
- **domain** (string) - Optional - The domain to use for the API request.
- **debug** (boolean) - Optional - Enable debug mode for the sandbox.
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **sandbox_id** (string) - The ID of the newly created sandbox.
- **template** (string) - The template used for the sandbox.
- **timeout** (integer) - The timeout duration for the sandbox in seconds.
- **metadata** (object) - Custom metadata associated with the sandbox.
- **envs** (object) - Environment variables set for the sandbox.
- **created_at** (string) - The timestamp when the sandbox was created (ISO 8601 format).
- **api_key** (string) - The API key used for the sandbox.
- **domain** (string) - The domain associated with the sandbox.
- **debug** (boolean) - Indicates if debug mode is enabled for the sandbox.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandbox_id": "sbx_abc123",
  "template": "base",
  "timeout": 300,
  "metadata": {},
  "envs": {},
  "created_at": "2023-10-27T10:00:00Z",
  "api_key": "pk_test_123",
  "domain": "e2b.dev",
  "debug": false
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new sandbox instance

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v1.6.0/sandbox/page.mdx

The static `create()` method on the `Sandbox` class provides a way to instantiate new sandbox environments. It can create a sandbox from the default 'desktop' template or a specified template by name or ID, with optional connection `opts`.

```typescript
static create<S>(this: S, opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>
```

```typescript
static create<S>(
   this: S, 
   template: string, 
opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>
```

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()

```

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create('<template-name-or-id>')

```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.8.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance. You can specify various configurations such as template, timeouts, environment variables, and metadata.

```APIDOC
## POST /e2b-api/sandboxes

### Description
Create a new sandbox. By default, the sandbox is created from the default `base` sandbox template.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/e2b-api/sandboxes

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **template** (string) - Optional - Sandbox template name or ID.
- **timeout** (integer) - Optional - Timeout for the sandbox in seconds, default to 300 seconds. Maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86_400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3_600 seconds) for Hobby users.
- **api_key** (string) - Optional - E2B API Key to use for authentication, defaults to `E2B_API_KEY` environment variable.
- **domain** (string) - Optional - The domain to use for the sandbox.
- **debug** (boolean) - Optional - Enable debug mode for the sandbox.
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.
- **proxy** (object) - Optional - Proxy to use for the request and for the requests made to the returned sandbox.
- **secure** (boolean) - Optional - If true, the sandbox is secured with an access token and cannot be used without it.

#### Request Body
- **metadata** (object) - Optional - Custom metadata for the sandbox.
- **envs** (object) - Optional - Custom environment variables for the sandbox.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **sandbox_id** (string) - The ID of the newly created sandbox.
- **...other sandbox details**

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandbox_id": "sandbox-xxxxxxxxxxxx",
  "template": "base",
  "created_at": "2023-10-27T10:00:00Z",
  "host": "sandbox.e2b.dev",
  "instance_type": "default"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create Beta Sandbox (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-python-sdk/v2.0.1/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance, similar to `create` but with an additional `auto_pause` option. This feature is in beta and may change.

```python
@classmethod
def beta_create(cls,
                template: Optional[str] = None,
                resolution: Optional[Tuple[int, int]] = None,
                dpi: Optional[int] = None,
                display: Optional[str] = None,
                timeout: Optional[int] = None,
                auto_pause: Optional[bool] = False,
                metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
                envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
                secure: bool = True,
                allow_internet_access: bool = True,
                **opts: Unpack[ApiParams]) -> Self
```

--------------------------------

### Create New Sandbox Instance (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.11.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Class method to create a new sandbox instance. Allows customization through parameters like template, timeout, metadata, environment variables, API key, proxy, and internet access. Defaults to the 'base' template if none is specified. Returns the created sandbox instance.

```python
@classmethod
async def create(cls, template: Optional[str] = None, timeout: Optional[int] = None, metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None, envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None, api_key: Optional[str] = None, domain: Optional[str] = None, debug: Optional[bool] = None, request_timeout: Optional[float] = None, proxy: Optional[ProxyTypes] = None, secure: Optional[bool] = None, allow_internet_access: Optional[bool] = True):
    # ... implementation details ...
    pass
```

--------------------------------

### Create a New Sandbox Instance - Python

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.3.2/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Initializes and creates a new E2B sandbox environment. Allows customization through template selection, timeout, metadata, environment variables, and authentication details. If no sandbox_id is provided, a new one is created.

```python
from e2b import Sandbox

# Example usage:
sandbox = Sandbox(template="<template_name>", timeout=600, metadata={"env": "dev"})
print(sandbox.sandbox_id)
```

--------------------------------

### E2B Sandbox: Create Sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.0.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance and automatically connects the terminal to it. Allows specifying a template and changing the root directory for command execution.

```bash
e2b sandbox create [options] [template]
```

--------------------------------

### Create New Sandbox Instance (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.2.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance using the `create` class method. Supports custom templates, timeouts, environment variables, and API key authentication. Use this instead of the constructor.

```python
await AsyncSandbox.create(template='my-template', timeout=600, envs={'VAR': 'value'}, api_key='your_api_key')
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new sandbox instance

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v1.6.1/sandbox/page.mdx

The `create` static method on the `Sandbox` class is used to instantiate a new sandbox environment. It can create a sandbox from the default template or a specified template, with optional connection options.

```typescript
static create<S>(this: S, opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>
```

```typescript
static create<S>(
   this: S, 
   template: string, 
opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>
```

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
```

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create('<template-name-or-id>')
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox from Default Template using TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.11.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Create a new sandbox instance from the default 'base' sandbox template. This method is asynchronous and returns a promise that resolves to the newly created sandbox instance.

```typescript
static create<S>(this: S, opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>

// Example:
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
```

--------------------------------

### Create New Sandbox (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.13.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Create a new sandbox instance. This function can create a sandbox from the default 'base' template or a specified custom template. It accepts optional sandbox configuration options and returns a Promise that resolves to the newly created sandbox instance.

```typescript
static create<S>(this: S, opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>

// Example:
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()

static create<S>(
   this: S, 
   template: string, 
opts?: SandboxOpts): Promise<InstanceType<S>>

// Example:
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create('<template-name-or-id>')
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox Instance

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.2/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance from a specified sandbox template. It takes the template name or ID and optional connection options. Returns a promise that resolves to the sandbox instance.

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create('<template-name-or-id>')
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new sandbox using Sandbox.create

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.0/sandbox/page.mdx

This function creates a new sandbox instance from a specified sandbox template. It accepts the template name or ID and optional connection options. The function returns a Promise that resolves to the sandbox instance.

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()

```

```typescript
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create('<template-name-or-id>')

```

### Create Sandbox Instance

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.6/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance from a specified template. This method is asynchronous and returns a Promise that resolves to the sandbox instance. It accepts optional connection options.

```typescript
static async create<S extends typeof Sandbox>(
   this: S,
   template: string,
   opts?: SandboxOpts
): Promise<InstanceType<S>> {
  // Implementation details...
  return null as any;
}
```

### Get Sandbox Metrics using CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox/metrics/page.mdx

Retrieves sandbox metrics (CPU, memory, disk usage) from the command line using the E2B CLI. This command requires the sandbox ID as an argument and outputs formatted metrics. The output includes timestamp, CPU usage percentage and core count, memory usage, and disk usage.

```bash
e2b sandbox metrics <sandbox_id>  # $HighlightLine

# Metrics for sandbox <sandbox_id>
# 
# [2025-07-25 14:05:55Z]  CPU:  8.27% /  2 Cores | Memory:    31 / 484   MiB | Disk:  1445 / 2453  MiB
# [2025-07-25 14:06:00Z]  CPU:   0.5% /  2 Cores | Memory:    32 / 484   MiB | Disk:  1445 / 2453  MiB
# [2025-07-25 14:06:05Z]  CPU:   0.1% /  2 Cores | Memory:    32 / 484   MiB | Disk:  1445 / 2453  MiB
# [2025-07-25 14:06:10Z]  CPU:   0.3% /  2 Cores | Memory:    32 / 484   MiB | Disk:  1445 / 2453  MiB
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Creation

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.7/sandbox/page.mdx

This section details how to create a new sandbox instance using the `create` method.

```APIDOC
## POST /sandbox/create

### Description
Creates a new sandbox instance from the default `base` sandbox template.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/sandbox/create

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **opts** (SandboxOpts) - Optional - Options for creating the sandbox.

### Request Example
```typescript
import { Sandbox } from 'e2b'

const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **sandboxInstance** (InstanceType<S>) - The created sandbox instance.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandboxId": "sandbox-12345"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create New Sandbox Instance (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.9.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance, optionally from a specified template and with custom configurations like environment variables, metadata, and timeouts. It supports API key authentication and proxy settings. This is the primary method for initializing a sandbox.

```python
@classmethod
async def create(cls, template: Optional[str] = None, timeout: Optional[int] = None, metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None, envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None, api_key: Optional[str] = None, domain: Optional[str] = None, debug: Optional[bool] = None, request_timeout: Optional[float] = None, proxy: Optional[ProxyTypes] = None, secure: Optional[bool] = None) -> 'AsyncSandbox':
    """
    Create a new sandbox.

    By default, the sandbox is created from the default `base` sandbox template.

    Arguments:
        template: Sandbox template name or ID
        timeout: Timeout for the sandbox in **seconds**, default to 300 seconds. Maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86_400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3_600 seconds) for Hobby users.
        metadata: Custom metadata for the sandbox
        envs: Custom environment variables for the sandbox
        api_key: E2B API Key to use for authentication, defaults to `E2B_API_KEY` environment variable
        request_timeout: Timeout for the request in **seconds**
        proxy: Proxy to use for the request and for the **requests made to the returned sandbox**
        secure: Envd is secured with access token and cannot be used without it

    Returns:
        sandbox instance for the new sandbox
    """
    pass
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Metrics API

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Fetches performance metrics for a sandbox, such as CPU, memory, and disk usage.

```APIDOC
## GET /sandboxes/{sandboxId}/metrics

### Description
Retrieves the performance metrics for a specified sandbox, including CPU, memory, and disk utilization.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/sandboxes/{sandboxId}/metrics

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **sandboxId** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the sandbox.

#### Query Parameters
- **opts** (SandboxMetricsOpts) - Optional - Options for specifying the type or range of metrics to retrieve.

### Request Example
None (GET request)

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **metrics** (SandboxMetrics[]) - An array of objects, each containing CPU, memory, and disk usage information for the sandbox.

#### Response Example
```json
[
  {
    "cpuUsage": 0.5,
    "memoryUsage": 0.7,
    "diskUsage": 0.3
  }
]
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create a New Sandbox Instance

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.6/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Initializes a new sandbox instance. It can be configured with a specific template, timeout, metadata, environment variables, API key, and request timeout. Defaults to the 'base' template if none is specified. The maximum sandbox runtime is limited by user tier.

```python
def __init__(template: Optional[str] = None,
             timeout: Optional[int] = None,
             metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
             envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
             api_key: Optional[str] = None,
             domain: Optional[str] = None,
             debug: Optional[bool] = None,
             sandbox_id: str = None,
             request_timeout: Optional[float] = None)
```
